mechanism_narrative: The Fed has held FEDFUNDS at 5.25% since June 2006, ending an 18-month tightening cycle. Equity markets price a pause as a reduction in future discount-rate risk, producing systematically positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns as long as the rate remains flat rather than rising.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% since August 2006 vintage
regime_hint: monetary policy pause after tightening cycle
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS-flat regimes are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) have declined sharply from their 2005-06 peak. A rapid contraction in HOUST signals weakening residential investment demand that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1795k (vintage) to 1737k revised by mid-2006
regime_hint: housing market downturn following construction boom
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in >55% of instances when HOUST decline threshold is triggered, or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a labor market inflection point that leads corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the combination captures the turning-point signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average m/m gain falls below 100k AND UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% and PAYEMS growth moderating in mid-2006
regime_hint: labor market transitioning from expansion to slowdown
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met jointly, 20-day forward SPX returns are non-negative in >55% of cases, or the joint signal's predictive power is not superior to either series individually (interaction term insignificant at p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion following monetary tightening, decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) foreshadows a broader earnings slowdown as capacity utilization and output growth roll over, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO growth pace moderating after multi-year expansion by 2006 vintage
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion following completed tightening cycle
failure_condition: INDPRO trend deceleration in this regime fails to precede negative 60-day SPX returns in >55% of observed late-cycle windows, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows restricted to late-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: After a prolonged plateau at the cycle-peak rate, the first month-over-month decrease in FEDFUNDS signals an imminent easing pivot, triggering a short-horizon rally as markets rapidly reprice discount rates lower.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases month-over-month after being flat for 3 or more consecutive months at the cycle peak
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.25% for three consecutive months as of August 2006 vintage
regime_hint: transition from monetary pause to easing
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following the first rate cut after a plateau are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one historical easing-pivot episode.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand, which supports corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon through a continuous momentum channel.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 368233 million in August 2006 reflecting continued consumer spending
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumer spending momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trend and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS momentum periods show no statistically significant excess return versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold from cycle-low levels marks the transition from expansion to contraction risk, historically preceding negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon as labor slack begins to erode consumer income growth.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% from a base below 4.7%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of August 2006 vintage near cycle lows
regime_hint: labor market at cyclical trough beginning to deteriorate
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following UNRATE crossing above 5.0% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the signal shows no lead time (crossing coincides with returns rather than preceding them).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts contract sharply while the federal funds rate remains elevated, restrictive monetary policy compounds housing-driven demand weakness, producing an amplified negative short-horizon equity reaction beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 10% AND FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST declining while FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% as of August 2006 vintage
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy coinciding with housing downturn
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns under the joint trigger are not more negative than under either single-series trigger condition alone (interaction effect insignificant at p>0.10), or positive returns occur in >50% of joint-trigger instances.
